stance: Quality-tilted risk-on within the hybrid mandate: lean into the cheap energy/materials value-plus-rising-revisions cluster, keep the bank/compounder core, prune the falling-revision tail.
experience_note: Watching whether the cheap-commodity value+rising-revision cluster (ONGC/NMDC/COALINDIA/HINDALCO/NATIONALUM/GRASIM) actually delivers over the next 1-2 quarters versus the quality compounders I de-sized; if commodity revisions roll over or crude/metal prices fall and these names' momentum turns negative while the de-sized private banks re-rate, my tilt was wrong. I would also reconsider if breadth collapses into mega-cap banks (the names I trimmed) leading the index. Pre-registered: I am NOT claiming the high-ARM names are guaranteed winners — IC is small; I expect roughly half to disappoint and am judging the BASKET, not any single name.
guardrail_notes: dropped 4 non-priceable/unknown ticker(s): ASTRAL, SHREECEM, OBEROIRLTY, MRF
